title: Kuratowski's Theorem: Characterizing Planar Graphs
description: A diagram showing the two fundamental non-planar graphs, K5 and K3,3, and how other non-planar graphs must contain a subdivision of one of these.
elements: Complete Graph K5 (5 vertices, all connected)
Complete Bipartite Graph K3,3 (two sets of 3 vertices, all possible connections between sets)
Example of a complex non-planar graph
Subdivision of K5 found within the complex graph
relationships: K5 and K3,3 are the minimal non-planar graphs (Kuratowski graphs)
The complex graph is non-planar because it contains a subdivision of K5
Any non-planar graph must contain a subdivision of either K5 or K3,3
